The unexpected ​guidance suggests the United States' best AI chips may have been making their way to the subsidiaries of Chinese AI firms based in places like Malaysia for almost a year despite broader U.S. efforts to starve Chinese firms of semiconductors needed to develop critical ‌AI capabilities.  The new guidance was ⁠posted on the Commerce Department's website on Sunday.
